text:

* orientation follows right-hand rule [2].

text: Forms functions take vectors input return scalars. 1-form takes single vector input, 2-form takes two vectors input, .definition (1-form = linear functional = covector = covariant vector) linear, real-valued function single vector input, \(\boldsymbol{\omega}: \mathbf{V} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}\), satisfies following conditions vectors scalars:(Additivity) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u} + \mathbf{v}) = \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}) + \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v})\).(Homogeneity) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(k\mathbf{v}) = k \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v})\).\(\mathbf{V}\) real vector space.\(\mathbb{R}\) set real numbers.\(\boldsymbol{\omega}\) 1-form.\(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}\) vectors \(\mathbf{V}\).\(k\) scalar.definition (2-form) bilinear, antisymmetric, real-valued function two vector inputs, \(\boldsymbol{\omega}: \mathbf{V} \times \mathbf{V} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}\), satisfies following conditions vectors scalars:(Bilinearity) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u} + b\mathbf{u}', \mathbf{v}) = \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}) + b \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}', \mathbf{v})\) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v} + b\mathbf{v}') = \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}) + b \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}')\).(Antisymmetry) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{v}) = -\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}, \mathbf{u})\).\(\mathbf{V}\) real vector space.\(\mathbb{R}\) set real numbers.\(\boldsymbol{\omega}\) 2-form.\(\mathbf{u}, \mathbf{u}', \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{v}'\) vectors \(\mathbf{V}\).\(, b\) scalars.definition (k-form) multilinear, completely antisymmetric, real-valued function \(k\) vector inputs, \dots, \mathbf{v}_k) = \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_i, \dots, \mathbf{v}_k) + b \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_i', \dots, \mathbf{v}_k)\) argument \(\) (k-Linearity) \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_i + b\mathbf{v}_i', \dots, \mathbf{v}_k) = \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_i, \dots, \mathbf{v}_k) + b \cdot \boldsymbol{\omega}(\mathbf{v}_1, \dots, \mathbf{v}_i', \dots, \mathbf{v}_k)\) argument \(\) \(1 \leq \leq k\).\(1 \leq \leq k\).(Complete Antisymmetry) Swapping two vector inputs reverses sign output: \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\dots, \mathbf{u}, \dots, \mathbf{v}, \dots) = -\boldsymbol{\omega}(\dots, \mathbf{v}, \dots, \mathbf{u}, \dots)\).(Complete Antisymmetry) Swapping two vector inputs reverses sign output: \(\boldsymbol{\omega}(\dots, \mathbf{u}, \dots, \mathbf{v}, \dots) = -\boldsymbol{\omega}(\dots, \mathbf{v}, \dots, \mathbf{u}, \dots)\).\(\mathbf{V}\) real chapter: 12 Stoke’s Theorem and the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
heading: 12 Stoke’s Theorem and the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
text: Stoke’s Theorem given ,\[\int_D d\omega = \int_{\partial D} \omega\]\(\omega\): differential \((k-1)\)-form.\(d\omega\): exterior derivative form \(\omega\).\(D\): \(k\)-dimensional domain integration.\(\partial D\): oriented boundary domain \(D\).

heading: 13 Symmetry
text:
definition (Symmetry) property tensor matrix components remain unchanged indices interchanged object transposed, satisfying following conditions:(Tensor Component Form) second-rank tensor \(\), symmetric \(A_{mn} = A_{nm}\) \(m\) \(n\).(Matrix Transposition) matrix symmetric unchanged transposition, \(\tilde{} = \).(Resolution Property) Every second-rank tensor can resolved symmetric part, given \(\frac{1}{2}(A_{mn} + A_{nm})\), antisymmetric part.
- \(\) tensor matrix.
- \(m, n\) indices identifying components.
- \(\tilde{}\) (\(^T\)) transpose matrix.definition (Antisymmetry) property tensor matrix components change sign indices interchanged, satisfying following conditions:(Tensor Component Form) second-rank tensor \(\), antisymmetric \(A_{mn} = -A_{nm}\) \(m\) \(n\).(Diagonal Property) antisymmetric matrix, diagonal elements (\(m=n\)) must zero.(Dual Association) 3-D space, every antisymmetric second-rank tensor \(C\) can associated dual pseudovector \(\mathbf{C}\).
- \(\) tensor matrix.
- \(m, n\) indices identifying components.
- \(C\) antisymmetric tensor.definition (Permutational Antisymmetry = Fermion Antisymmetry) property identical-particle systems, specifically fermions, whose wave functions must change sign pairwise particle interchanges, satisfying following conditions:(Permutation Condition) many-fermion wave function \(\Psi_F(1, \dots, n)\) must satisfy \(P\Psi_F(1, \dots, n) = \epsilon_P\Psi_F(1, \dots, n)\) permutation \(P\).(Representation Role) \(\Psi_F\) serves sole basis function totally antisymmetric representation symmetric group \(S_n\).(Levi-Civita Relation) sign transformation determined Levi-Civita symbol \(\epsilon_P\), \(\epsilon_P = 1\) even permutations \(\epsilon_P = -1\) odd permutations.
- \(\Psi_F\) fermion wave function.
- \(P\) permutation operator acting particle numbers.
- \(\epsilon_P\) parity permutation.
- \(S_n\) symmetric group order \(n!\).
